title: Linkia prostrata
intro: Linkia prostrata, also known as the creeping daisy or creeping groundsel, is a flowering plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health, with potential benefits including the relief of coughs, colds, and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Linkia prostrata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Linkia prostrata is classified within the phylum Echinodermata, specifically within the class Asteroidea, which includes starfish. It belongs to the family Oreasteridae, commonly known as porcellanasterids. These starfish are typically found in tropical and subtropical waters around the world. The family Oreasteridae is characterized by their distinctive starfish shape and the ability to regenerate lost arms.
